metadata.archetype_justification: Frontend-only Angular SPA with no backend, no database connections, no write operations to any owned data store, and no server-side API surface. All data operations are delegated to an external API. Classified as stateless-utility.
metadata.context: Angular reference implementation of the RealWorld spec.

evaluations.reason: archetype-N/A: stateless-utility, sync HTTP is correct design
surface-gated: no deployed workload + no API surface
archetype-N/A: stateless-utility, sync is correct design
No unstructured data managed by this frontend application
No database in this frontend application
surface-gated: no API surface for SLOs

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No infrastructure-as-code exists in this repository. Hosting infrastructure (Netlify) is configured entirely outside the codebase with no Terraform, CDK, CloudFormation, or Helm definitions.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. Infrastructure is manually configured (ClickOps on Netlify). No reproducible infrastructure definition exists.
findings.recommendation: Define hosting infrastructure in Terraform or CDK: S3 bucket for static assets, CloudFront distribution with OAC, Route 53 DNS records, ACM certificate for TLS.

findings.title: Partial CI/CD — Build Automated but Deployment Manual
findings.description: GitHub Actions workflows exist for build, lint, E2E testing, and security testing. However, deployment to production hosting is not controlled by this pipeline — it creates GitHub Release artifacts but does not deploy them.
findings.gap: Build is automated but deployment is not controlled by the CI/CD pipeline. No IaC deployment automation exists.
findings.recommendation: Extend GitHub Actions to deploy build artifacts to AWS (S3 upload + CloudFront invalidation). Add environment promotion with approval gates.

findings.title: Single Deployable Monolith SPA
findings.description: The application is a single deployable unit — one Angular build produces one bundle. While feature directories exist, they are not independently deployable. All features share global state, a single router, and produce a single build artifact.
findings.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith with no independent deployment capability. All features must be built and deployed together.
findings.recommendation: For a reference/demo app of this size, full decomposition is not warranted. If independent deployment is desired: adopt Angular Module Federation for per-feature-module builds.

findings.title: API Versioning via Spec Only
findings.description: The consumed API has an OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification (v2.0.0) but no explicit version segment in the URL path. Version is tracked in spec metadata but not enforced in the URL structure.
findings.gap: No explicit version segment in API URL path (e.g., /v1/articles). Version changes could break the frontend without warning.
findings.recommendation: Pin OpenAPI spec version and add contract testing to detect breaking changes from the external API.

findings.title: Hard-coded API Endpoint
findings.description: The external API endpoint is hard-coded in the api interceptor as 'https://api.realworld.show/api'. No environment variable configuration or runtime config injection exists.
findings.gap: Cannot switch between environments (dev, staging, production) without code changes and rebuilds.
findings.recommendation: Externalize the API base URL using Angular environment files or runtime configuration injection (e.g., /assets/config.json loaded at startup).

findings.title: No Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging is configured. No infrastructure exists in this repository to enable audit logging.
findings.gap: No audit logging infrastructure. User actions are not logged in any audit trail controlled by this repository.
findings.recommendation: When migrating to AWS hosting: enable CloudTrail, configure CloudFront access logging to S3, and add CloudWatch RUM for client-side event logging.

findings.title: Client-side JWT Without OAuth2/OIDC
findings.description: JWT token-based authentication is implemented via localStorage and token interceptor. No OAuth2/OIDC flow, no API Gateway authorizer, no Cognito integration.
findings.gap: No OAuth2/OIDC flow. Token stored in localStorage (XSS-vulnerable). Authentication relies entirely on external API token issuance.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito for OAuth2/OIDC-based authentication with PKCE. Use httpOnly cookies for token management to mitigate XSS risk.

findings.title: No Centralized Identity Provider
findings.description: The application manages its own authentication entirely with no external IdP integration. Login/register flows send credentials directly to the backend API with no Cognito, Okta, SAML, or OIDC federation.
findings.gap: No centralized IdP integration. No SSO capability. Authentication is entirely self-managed.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito as identity provider. Use Cognito Hosted UI for login/register flows with OAuth2/OIDC, enabling SSO, social login, and MFA.

findings.title: Secrets in GitHub Actions Without Rotation
findings.description: CI/CD secrets are stored in GitHub Actions secrets (AWS credentials, API keys). No AWS Secrets Manager, no Vault, no rotation configured.
findings.gap: No dedicated secrets management service with rotation. Static AWS access keys in GitHub secrets without OIDC federation.
findings.recommendation: Use OIDC federation for GitHub Actions → AWS (eliminates static access keys). Use AWS Secrets Manager for any service credentials with automated rotation.

findings.title: No Compute Hardening or Vulnerability Scanning
findings.description: No compute hardening or patching strategy exists. No server-side compute under this repo's control. No vulnerability scanning (Inspector, Snyk) configured.
findings.gap: No vulnerability scanning of the deployment environment. No container image scanning, no SSM Patch Manager.
findings.recommendation: Focus on dependency vulnerability scanning in CI (npm audit, Dependabot). If containerized in future, use ECR image scanning and Bottlerocket base images.

findings.title: No SAST or Dependency Scanning in CI
findings.description: Security-focused E2E tests exist (XSS testing via Playwright) but no SAST tool, no Dependabot, and no npm audit in the CI pipeline.
findings.gap: No SAST tool. No dependency vulnerability scanning. Security tests limited to behavioral XSS testing.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot for automated dependency alerts. Add npm audit --audit-level=critical as a blocking CI step. Consider Semgrep for SAST.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: No distributed tracing instrumented. No OpenTelemetry SDK, no X-Ray, no trace ID propagation. Cannot correlate frontend requests with backend processing.
findings.gap: No tracing instrumentation. No visibility into request latency breakdown or API call correlation.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry browser SDK or AWS CloudWatch RUM for frontend performance monitoring and trace context propagation.

findings.title: No Business Metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics published. No analytics, no CloudWatch custom metrics, no user engagement tracking.
findings.gap: Cannot measure user engagement, article creation rates, or authentication success rates.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch RUM for real user monitoring. Instrument key user journeys with custom metrics.

findings.title: No Anomaly Detection or Alerting
findings.description: No anomaly detection or alerting configured. No CloudWatch alarms, no error rate monitoring, no latency alerts.
findings.gap: No automated notification for API unavailability or deployment failures.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch alarms on CloudFront error rates. Add CloudWatch Synthetics for critical user journey canaries.

findings.title: No Staged Deployment Strategy
findings.description: Deployment is a rolling update via Netlify git integration — push to main triggers immediate production deployment. No canary, blue/green, or staged rollout.
findings.gap: No ability to test changes on a subset of users. No rollback mechanism beyond git revert.
findings.recommendation: Adopt CloudFront with weighted routing for canary deployments. Deploy new versions to secondary S3 prefix with gradual traffic shifting.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no incident response automation, no self-healing patterns. Incident response is entirely ad hoc.
findings.gap: No documented procedures for common failure scenarios. No automated remediation.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common incidents (API downtime, deployment rollback, CDN cache invalidation). When on AWS, use SSM Automation documents.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: No observability ownership defined. No service-level dashboards, no alarms with named owners, no CODEOWNERS for monitoring assets.
findings.gap: No one explicitly responsible for monitoring application health.
findings.recommendation: Define CODEOWNERS covering observability configs. Create per-service dashboards with named owners and team tags.

findings.title: No Resource Tagging Governance
findings.description: No AWS resource tagging exists because no AWS resources are defined. No tagging standard, no tag policies, no cost allocation tags.
findings.gap: No tagging governance for future AWS resources.
findings.recommendation: Establish a tagging standard (Environment, Service, Owner, CostCenter) and enforce via Terraform default_tags or CDK Aspects when provisioning AWS resources.

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC — all infrastructure is configured manually outside the repository.
top_gaps.impact: Cannot reproduce environments, no disaster recovery, no infrastructure audit trail.

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Single-page application is a tightly-coupled frontend monolith.
top_gaps.impact: Cannot independently scale or deploy features.

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging configured.

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity Integration
top_gaps.gap: Application manages own JWT authentication with no external IdP.
top_gaps.impact: Inconsistent access policies, no SSO capability.

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing or observability instrumentation.
top_gaps.impact: Cannot diagnose request-flow issues or API call failures.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Knowledge agent that indexes existing documentation and OpenAPI spec for developer questions
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index README, CLAUDE.md, and OpenAPI spec for onboarding queries

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 2, GitHub Actions workflows in place)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that triggers builds, checks workflow status, and manages releases
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Add structured deployment outputs for enhanced agent surface

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Reference/demo app with < 60 source files. Angular feature module structure provides natural seams but current scale does not justify full decomposition. Prioritize infrastructure automation first.

effort_factors.module_boundaries: Low effort - clear feature directories with lazy-loaded routes
effort_factors.data_coupling: Medium effort - shared UserService global state
effort_factors.ci_cd_maturity: Medium effort - build exists, deploy is manual
effort_factors.test_coverage: Low effort - E2E tests cover critical workflows

detail.current_state: Single Angular SPA monolith deployed to Netlify with no IaC

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No deployed workload exists; static SPA deployed to Netlify. Containerization is not the correct modernization path for a static frontend.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1 not evaluated (no deployed workload). No container definitions found but containerization is not applicable for static SPA.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: This is a static SPA with no server-side compute. The correct hosting model is CDN (CloudFront+S3), not containers.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No commercial database engines detected. Frontend-only application with no database.

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures or proprietary SQL — score meets threshold
